descript.abstract: This data set contains road inventory for the state of Arkansas. This file contains roads that pertain to state and federal funding and additional roads used for ARDOT planning purposes. This file should only be used as a graphical representation and general reporting. Field verification should always occur prior to financial planning taking place.
descript.purpose: This file is used for reporting road inventory to the US Department of Transportation.

idinfo.useconst: Individuals or companies may reproduce any maps prepared by the Planning and Research Division (if available to the public) for their own use and can utilize the data for other purposes. In all cases, a credit line stating "Data Provided by Arkansas Department of Transportation, In Cooperation With The U.S. Department of Transportation"
idinfo.native: Microsoft Windows 7 Version 6.1 (Build 7601) Service Pack 1; Esri ArcGIS 10.3.0.4322

attr.attrdef: Is a five digit numeric code that indicates which urbanized area a record belongs in. These codes are assigned by FHWA and are unique within the United States.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.
attr.attrdefs: ESRI

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Codes to indicate open/closed status of a roadway. Roadways not open to traffic are either projected/planned or routes under construction.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: A code used to distinguish the type of record. This is a key field and must be used to select the type of records you wish to view or query
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

edom.edomvds: Short description of roadway features – limited to 65 characters
Features, lengths, and log miles of a section of highway
Gives log mile and railroad ID number of R/R crossing
A section of roadway is carried over another section of roadway
Department use only denotes curves from construction plans
Construction job information job number, completion date, etc…
Gives log mile of bridge length structure (>= 20’) with bridge #

attr.attrdef: County Name
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: A value used to make each record key unique. Alphanumeric characters range A through Z. Sequence is also used to order records at the same Logmile point.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: Codes used to designate routes on the National Highway System.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: The beginning point of a particular record on a route.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: The beginning point of a particular record on a route. Begin log mile is numeric and allows for 6 digits, 3 to the left of the decimal and 3 to the right of the decimal.
attr.attrdefs: Arkansas Highway and Transportation Department

attr.attrdef: Feature geometry.
attr.attrdefs: ESRI

attr.attrdef: The number of the highway district in which the county is located.
attr.attrdefs: Arkansas Highway and Transportation Department

attr.attrdef: Used to identify the level of government that owns or is responsible for the section of roadway.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: Codes used to designate routes on the APHN. Routes are used in planning studies and fall into five categories.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

edom.edomvd: Critical Service Routes (These routes parallel the Interstate system)
edom.edomvds: Arkansas Primary Highway Network

edom.edomvd: Other Arterials (functionally classified, 3 or 4 and not on NHS
edom.edomvds: Arkansas Primary Highway Network

edom.edomvd: Other High Traffic Routes- (Designated routes with ADT over 2,000)
edom.edomvds: Arkansas Primary Highway Network
Arkansas Primary Highway Network
Arkansas Primary Highway Network

attr.attrdef: Average Daily Traffic Count
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: The number assigned to the county when placed in alphabetical order. County is a numeric value from 01 to 75.
attr.attrdefs: Arkansas Highway and Transportation Department

attr.attrdef: Unique Id
The section number of a particular section or sub-section of a route. Section is a 3 character alpha numeric identifier that always leads with a numeric value.
attr.attrdefs: Arkansas Highway and Transportation Department

attr.attrdef: Identifies the HPMS sample by a 12-digit sample identifier. Used for HPMS reporting only. ATTENTION! Only HPMS personnel are allowed to update or modify this field.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: Numeric identifier assigned to Maintenance crews responsible for the particular segment of road. This field is protected and only Maintenance personnel can update or modify this field.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: Type of median on a divided roadway.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

edom.edomvds: Trees, rolling hills, rock walls, etc
Grass median
Flexible cable barriers
No median is present
Concrete barrier wall
Barrier curb

attr.attrdef: This field is used for street names, river or stream names, and other pertinent roadway information of a descriptive nature.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: The year of last reconstruction or overlay (if documented)
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: Actual measured median width in feet. If median exceeds 1,000 feet or a width is impossible to attain then 999 will be entered into the field as the width.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: Indicates the presence and type of lanes other than through traffic lanes.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

edom.edomvd: Auxiliary Lanes (Typically connect the ramps of two interchanges)
Combination (Center Turn Lane and Parking Lanes)

attr.attrdef: The total actual measured width of the roadway surface including, through travel lanes, extra lanes, and shoulders. Excludes the median width.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: Is a concatenation of County, Route, and Section that is separated by a lowercase “x” EX:60x00100x010 is County: 60, Route: 00100, and SECTION: 010. This is now the key field for dynamic segmentation. County, Route, and Section are no longer used to segment the roadway inventory database.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: This field indicates the actual number of through traffic lanes on a roadway. Center turn lanes, auxiliary lanes, and other lanes not considered through travel lanes and not shown in number of lanes. However, passing lanes 500’ or longer are considered through travel lanes and are included.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: A code denoting the general physical features of the land. This field is for HPMS reporting and is only required in rural areas.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: The actual measurement in feet of the right shoulder.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: Codes used to identify the right shoulder surface. These codes and surface types follow FHWA requirements for reporting surface types and are not meant to be used for in-depth pavement analysis.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: The total actual measurement in feet for the through traffic lanes and extra lanes (except parking lanes). Does not include shoulder or median widths.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: The year of original construction (if documented).
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: A value used to make each record key unique. Alphanumeric characters range A through Z. Sequence is also used to order records at the same Logmile point.
attr.attrdefs: Arkansas Highway and Transportation Department

attr.attrdef: The length of a particular mileage record on a route. It is a calculated field that subtracts the begin log mile from the end log mile to produce a road length for the record.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: Indicates whether a section of roadway is a HPMS sample section. This field is used for HPMS reporting only.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

edom.edomvd: Donut & HPMS sample section (non-attainment areas only)

attr.attrdef: Year Average Daily Traffic Count
attr.attrdefs: Arkansas Highway and Transportation Department

attr.attrdef: The actual measurement in feet of the left shoulder.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: Codes used to identify the left shoulder surface. These codes and surface types follow FHWA requirements for reporting surface types and are not meant to be used for in-depth pavement analysis.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: The actual measurement in feet for the minimum traffic lane. Measurements are taken from the edge line to the center line. Normal traffic usage will be measured if no markings are present.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: Is a numeric character that identifies the rural/urban characteristics of a record.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

edom.edomvds: Population 50,000 to 199,999
Population 5,000 to 49,999
Population >=200,000
Population < 5,000

attr.attrdef: Reflects the operational traffic flow on a roadway.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: Codes used to identify the roadway surface. These codes and surface types follow FHWA requirements for reporting surface types and are not meant to be used for in-depth pavement analysis.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

edom.edomvd: Bridge Deck Other- only where bridge is carried as mileage
Bridge Deck Asphalt - only where bridge is carried as mileage
Bridge Deck Concrete - only where bridge is carried as mileage
Other (usually chip seal or surface treatment)
Asphalt overlay over existing Asphalt pavement

attr.attrdef: The section number of a particular section or sub-section of a route.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

edom.edomvds: Seldom used often signed as a Bus. Route
Part of a one-way couplet usually anti-log direction
Seldom used indicated direction of alternate route

edom.edomvd: Seldom used indicated direction of alternate route
edom.edomvds: Section is a 3 character alpha numeric identifier that always leads with a numeric value.
Seldom used indicates truck route
Seldom used indicated direction of alternate route
Used for projected routes only
Primary business route
Used only when two business routes exist on same route
Seldom used indicated direction of alternate route
Spur Route usually does not junction with another SH
Y leg of a route
Used only for projected routes that are planned but not built

attr.attrdef: Date record was updated
attr.attrdefs: Arkansas Highway and Transportation Department

attr.attrdef: Route number or name assigned to a particular State Highway, County Road, or
attr.attrdefs: Arkansas Highway and Transportation Department

attr.attrdef: The ending point of a particular record on a route.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: A single numeric character identifies the functional classification of a record
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: The number assigned to the county when placed in alphabetical order.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: Describes the type of roadway a section and separate the many varieties of roadway types.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

edom.edomvds: Eliminates weaving and reduces number of entrance and exit points
Default value for data entry
On-Off ramps from grade separated interchange
On right side of facility going log direction
Facility that connects one way frontage roads
Non-log direction divided or one-way roadways only
On left side of facility going log direction
Rest areas, weigh stations, other ARDOT facilities
Log Direction most records will be main lane

attr.attrdef: Describes how the route is signed
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

edom.edomvds: Includes, Interstate, Interstate frontage roads and ramps
US Numbered highways, frontage roads and ramps
Streets contained by the city limits or urban area boundary
County Roads- may or may not be maintained by the County
All other State maintained hwys, frontage roads and ramps

attr.attrdef: The number of the highway district in which the county is located.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: A five digit numeric code assigned to identify a particular place or city in Arkansas
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

attr.attrdef: Route number or name assigned to a particular State Highway, County Road, or City Street.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

edom.edomvd: County Roads are alphanumeric and 5 characters in length, all County Roads will have a leading alpha character followed by leading zeros
City Streets are 6 alphanumeric characters in length and begin with direction first
State Highway routes are numeric and 5 digits in length with leading zeros

attr.attrdef: Identifies a special road system.
attr.attrdefs: United States Roadway Inventory User Mannual

edom.edomvd: Institutional Road- Prisons, Collages, Vo-Tech’s, and Experimental stations

attr.attrdef: User who updated the record
attr.attrdefs: Arkansas Highway and Transportation Department

distinfo.distliab: See access and use constraints information.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
